text:
BOTTOM LINE — Broad risk-on, semis led the rebound.
It was a high-conviction recovery in growth after the Broadcom-led tech reset, with semiconductors, memory, and AI names driving the tape while defensives stayed bid. The close looked like positioning repair rather than macro relief: more volume in SPY/QQQ, lower VIX, and a broad-but-still-tech-led bounce. Rotations into healthcare, financials, and energy kept the rally from becoming purely one-factor momentum.

INDICES — VIX 19.4, down.
SOXX +8.39%, QQQ +3.38%, IWM +2.96%, DIA +1.82%, SPY +1.70%. Small > large by 0.42pp. Gold was the biggest cross-asset move at +3.13%.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 2.1x and QQQ vol_ratio 2.0x, both pointing to accumulation.
XLK drew attention at 1.2x volume while XLP 1.5x and XLI 1.4x were also active; XLU 0.9x and the 1.0x clusters in XLV/XLB were comparatively ignored. Price and volume were aligned in SPY/QQQ, so the index move had sponsorship rather than being a low-conviction bounce.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +3.73%, XLB Materials +3.27%, XLI Industrials +3.24%, XLY Discretionary +2.48%, XLC Comms +1.00%, XLV Healthcare +0.81%, XLF Financials +0.75%, XLU Utilities +0.11%, XLRE Real Estate -0.16%, XLP Staples -0.26%, XLE Energy -1.94%.
Top industries were DRAM +13.51% and REMX +9.20%; bottom industries were USO -4.07% and UNG -3.29%.
Money flowed into semis, cyclicals, and parts of value, while energy and defensives lagged; that reads as broadening participation off a tech reset, not a clean defensive bid. AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.07 and IWM PCR 1.18 both show protection demand, while QQQ PCR 0.95 is only mildly defensive.
SPY skew +8.7% and QQQ skew +11.1% keep put premium elevated, but VIX at 19.4 remains above its 20-day 17.6, so fear is still priced.

CROSS-ASSET — GLD +3.13% and SLV +5.48% rose while UUP -0.36% slipped, a classic softer-dollar impulse.
USO -4.07% fell against the equity rebound, signaling the oil bid was not broad-based and did not sustain energy leadership.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63.8/100, with SOXX +8.39% and QQQ +3.38% leading and XLE -1.94% lagging.
The cleanest vol spike was ADBE at 3.4x on -6.25%, which fits distribution, while ORCL at 2.8x on -8.53% also signals active selling. Watch ADBE -6.25% for post-spike follow-through, ORCL -8.53% for cloud weakness after heavy volume, and IWM +2.96% for whether small-cap leadership holds into next session.
